raw_response:
I agree with the interpretation provided. When we see a p-value of 0.00, it indicates overwhelming evidence against the null hypothesis, suggesting a near-zero probability that our findings resulted from random variation rather than some underlying effect. This level of significance strongly supports rejecting the null hypothesis in favor of the alternative, making option C ("1% of significance") accurate because it reflects the common threshold used to denote statistical significance (typically set at α = 0.05).

Therefore, my answer remains **C**.
